title: Stash Conflict Recovery
description: You stashed some in-progress work, pulled in a colleague's changes, and now `git stash pop` has exploded with conflicts. The stash needs to be applied and the conflicts resolved without losing either set of changes.
objective: The stash must be fully applied and all conflicts resolved. The file `config.js` must contain both the stashed change (a `timeout` setting) and the colleague's change (a `retries` setting). The working tree must be clean and the stash list must be empty. Commit the resolved file so the working tree is clean.
hints: `git stash pop` applies and drops the stash in one step — if it conflicts, the stash is NOT dropped automatically.
After resolving the conflict in the file, remove the conflict markers and `git add` the file.
After resolving and staging the file, commit it: `git commit -m "merge: resolve stash conflict"`.
Check `git stash list` — you may need to manually drop the stash entry after resolving.
